summary.intent: Address PR review comments on a branch that separates datetime injection from stable system prompts, fixing the Anthropic provider's handling of multiple system messages.
summary.outcome: Reverted an incorrect fix that would have caused consecutive user messages (invalid for Anthropic's API), kept Anthropic merging all system messages into the top-level system field, added a test, and pushed the corrected commit.

repo: Anthropic provider merges all system messages into a single top-level `system` field via `to_anthropic_messages`
The datetime injection system message is inserted before the user message in the message array, not appended after history
KV cache stability for local LLMs is the primary goal of the PR; Anthropic prompt caching via cache_control annotations is a separate, unimplemented feature

code.finding: to_anthropic_messages merges all ChatMessage::System variants into the top-level system field; converting subsequent system messages to user-role messages causes consecutive user message errors rejected by Anthropic's API

workflow: Anthropic's API requires strictly alternating user/assistant messages — consecutive user messages are rejected
When fixing review comments, verify API constraints before applying a seemingly clean refactor

friction: Initial fix (converting trailing system messages to user messages) violated Anthropic's alternating message requirement, requiring a second reverting edit
No existing tests for to_anthropic_messages meant the constraint wasn't caught earlier
open_items: Anthropic prompt caching via cache_control breakpoints is not implemented — system message stability for Anthropic's KV cache is deferred
The datetime injection still gets concatenated into Anthropic's system field on every request, defeating cache stability for Anthropic specifically
